pokemon:nosepass.name: Nosepass
pokemon:nosepass.summary: Nosepass had been said to be completely unmoving, with its magnetic nose pointed due north. However, close observation has revealed that the Pokémon actually moves by a little over 3/8 of an inch every year.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Nosepass had been said to be completely unmoving, with its magnetic nose pointed due north. However, close observation has revealed that the Pokémon actually moves by a little over 3/8 of an inch every year.
When endangered, it may protect itself by raising its magnetism and drawing iron objects to its body.
Its magnetic nose always faces north and draws iron objects to its body to protect itself better.
Its nose is a magnet. As a result, this Pokémon always keeps its face pointing north.
Its body emits a powerful magnetism. It feeds on prey that is pulled in by the force. Its magnetism is stronger in cold seasons.
Its magnetic nose consistently faces north. Travelers check NOSEPASS to get their bearings.
If two of these meet, they cannot get too close because their noses repel each other.
Its magnetic nose consistently faces north. Travelers check NOSEPASS to get their bearings.
Once the people of Hisui discovered that its red nose always points north, they grew to rely on it greatly when traveling afar. The nose seems to work in a similar way to ancient compasses.
It uses powerful magnetism to drag its prey toward it. It’s also been known to pull in metal, which it collects and uses to protect itself.
Nosepass’s magnetic nose is always pointed to the north. If two of these Pokémon meet, they cannot turn their faces to each other when they are close because their magnetic noses repel one another.
Its nose is a magnet. As a result, this Pokémon always keeps its face pointing north.
When endangered, it may protect itself by raising its magnetism and drawing iron objects to its body.
NOSEPASS’s magnetic nose is always pointed to the north. If two of these POKéMON meet, they cannot turn their faces to each other when they are close because their magnetic noses repel one another.

NOSEPASS had been said to be completely unmoving, with its magnetic nose pointed due north. However, close observation has revealed that the POKéMON actually moves by a little over 3/8 of an inch every year.
If two of these meet, they cannot get too close because their noses repel each other.
The magnet in Nosepass’s nose provides an unerring compass, making it an excellent partner for Trainers going on a journey.
It hunts without twitching a muscle by pulling in its prey with powerful magnetism. But sometimes it pulls natural enemies in close.
It moves less than an inch a year, but when it’s in a jam, it will spin and drill down into the ground in a split second.
When endangered, it may protect itself by raising its magnetism and drawing iron objects to its body.
Its magnetic nose always faces north and draws iron objects to its body to protect itself better.
Its magnetic nose always faces north and draws iron objects to its body to protect itself better.
Its magnetic nose consistently faces north. Travelers check Nosepass to get their bearings.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Nosepass had been said to be completely unmoving, with its magnetic nose pointed due north. However, close observation has revealed that the Pokémon actually moves by a little over 3/8 of an inch every year.

pokemon:probopass.name: Probopass
pokemon:probopass.summary: It freely controls three small units called Mini-Noses using magnetic force.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: It freely controls three small units called Mini-Noses using magnetic force.
It freely controls three small units called Mini-Noses using magnetic force.
It freely controls three units called Mini-Noses using magnetic force.
It exudes strong magnetism from all over. It controls three small units called Mini-Noses.
It controls three units called Mini-Noses using magnetic force. With them, it can attack the foe from three directions.
It is able to emit powerful magnetism, allowing it control over the iron sand that forms its luscious mustache. Using this iron sand, Probopass forms hard stones with which it smites its prey.
The main body controls three mobile units called Mini-Noses, which it maneuvers to catch prey.
It exudes strong magnetism from all over. It controls three small units called Mini-Noses.
It exudes strong magnetism from all over. It controls three small units called Mini-Noses.
It freely controls three small units called Mini-Noses using magnetic force.
It controls three units called Mini-Noses using magnetic force. With them, it can attack the foe from three directions.
It radiates such a powerful magnetic field that nearby electrical appliances become unusable.
It uses three small units to catch prey and battle enemies. The main body mostly just gives orders.
Although it can control its units known as Mini-Noses, they sometimes get lost and don’t come back.
It freely controls three small units called Mini-Noses using magnetic force.
It freely controls three units called Mini-Noses using magnetic force.
It exudes strong magnetism from all over. It controls three small units called Mini-Noses.
It freely controls three small units called Mini-Noses using magnetic force.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: It freely controls three small units called Mini-Noses using magnetic force.
